exemplar.title: How does the exposure level of 4 on Castle Beach and 6 on Jetty Beach based on the Ballantineâs scale affect the width-to-height ratio of Steromphala umbilicalis in June 2024?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the exposure level of 4 on Castle Beach and 6 on Jetty Beach based on the Ballantineâs scale affect the width-to-height ratio of Steromphala umbilicalis in June 2024?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question is precisely stated and embedded within a clear ecological and geographical context, facilitating focused investigation.
Detailed apparatus list includes instrument uncertainties, supporting full reproducibility.
Use of stratified random sampling and comprehensive control of variables is well justified and reduces bias.
Comprehensive risk assessment demonstrates robust planning for field safety.
weaknesses: Parameters of the random number generator (seed, range) are not specified, limiting exact reproducibility.
Software version and exact random point distribution method are not stated.
Grid reference for Castle Beach is omitted, weakening locational precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands fall within the 5â6 band: the research question is situated in a specific context (band 6), methodological considerations are explained with clear reasoning (band 5), and the methodology description allows reproduction with minimal ambiguity (band 5). Minor details (random generator specifics, software version, one grid reference) prevent absolute perfection, but the overall depth and clarity justify the maximum holistic mark of 6.

strengths: Data recording and processing are communicated both clearly and precisely with well-labelled tables and informative graphs.
Statistical analyses (ratios, means, standard deviations, two-sample t-test) are calculated and interpreted correctly.
Qualitative observations are effectively integrated with quantitative findings to support the narrative.
weaknesses: Instrument uncertainty (Â±0.05 mm) is stated but not propagated into ratio calculations or shown in error bars.
Inconsistent numeric formatting (comma vs decimal point) reduces readability.
Referenced data (e.g. Table for Figure 12) is missing and there is a transcription error in degrees of freedom.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication and processing of data are clear and largely precise (band 5). While there is evidence of considering uncertainties, significant omissions in uncertainty propagation place that strand in band 3. Data processing quality meets band 5 standards. Overall performance sits solidly in the 5â6 band but falls short of full precision in uncertainty treatment, so a score of 5 is warranted.

strengths: The conclusion is justified and directly answers the research question, referencing the numerical difference and t-test outcome.
Relevant comparison to accepted scientific context (limpet hydrodynamics and shell variation) is made.
weaknesses: Difference is mislabelled with units (âmmâ instead of a dimensionless ratio).
Literature comparison is brief and could be deepened with additional contrasting studies.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is fully consistent with the analysis and justified (band 5) but the scientific context comparison, while relevant, remains superficial (band 4). On balance, the overall performance aligns with the middle of the 3â4 band, meriting a score of 4.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified with clear explanations of their relative impacts on results.
Realistic and relevant improvements are proposed and explicitly linked to each identified limitation.
Thoughtful reflection on statistical certainty (95% confidence) demonstrates strong critical evaluation.
weaknesses: Some improvement suggestions lack detailed practicability considerations (e.g., resource or time constraints).
Feasibility details for alternative high-exposure sites could be further elaborated.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strandsâidentification of methodological weaknesses and proposed improvementsâare addressed with explained relevance and impact, meeting the top-band descriptors (band 5). Minor gaps in practicability details prevent the award of a 6, so a holistic score of 5 is appropriate.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student draws a **justified conclusion** that directly answers the research question, cites numerical difference (0.12) and refers to the t-test outcome, all fully consistent with the presented analysis.  A slight over-statement of certainty (uses âmmâ instead of ratio units) prevents awarding the maximum 6.

Raw data are presented in an extensive, labelled table with uncertainties, and processed values appear in a separate column.  Graphs (bar, box-whisker, histogram) include titles, axes and error bars.  Comma vs decimal-point inconsistencies and some cramped formatting reduce precision slightly, but overall the communication is **both clear and largely precise**.

Ratios, means, standard deviations and a two-sample t-test are calculated correctly and interpreted appropriately.  The choice of statistical test is justified and underlying assumptions (normality) briefly addressed.  Minor transcription issues in the t-table prevent a flawless score, so a 5 is appropriate.

A dedicated evaluation table lists six **specific methodological weaknesses** (limited exposure range, variable shelter, calliper error, tidal timing, height accuracy, inaccessible samples) and **explains** how each could bias results, including references to SD inflation or sample representativeness.  The depth of causal discussion aligns with the top band, though one or two impacts are only briefly stated, so mark 5.

The conclusion is compared to accepted scientific context through discussion of limpet hydrodynamics and nutrient-driven shell variation, demonstrating **relevant comparison**.  However, the comparison remains relatively brief and does not explore contrasting findings or multiple sources in depth, so the middle bandâs upper mark (4) is suitable.

The research question is stated in a focused, quantitative form that identifies the organism, two precise locations, a dated sampling period and the response variable (width-to-height ratio).  The introduction embeds the question within a **clearly specified ecological and geographical context** (Ballantine exposure scale, local geology, tidal zone, known shell dimensions from literature).  Consequently the assessor can fully understand why the question matters and how it fits existing knowledge, satisfying the descriptor for the top band.

Instrument uncertainty is stated (Â±0.05 mm) yet not propagated into the ratio or reflected in the error bars (which show SD only).  No discussion of uncertainty in t-test results or sampling error appears.  Thus there is **evidence of consideration** but with notable omissions, fitting the 3-4 band; the depth merits a 3.

For every limitation, the student proposes a **realistic, relevant improvement** (e.g., alternative higher-exposure site, shorter transect, digital callipers, pre-marking Chart Datum).  The link between each weakness and its improvement is explicitly justified, meeting the descriptor of âexplainedâ.  Minor practicability details could be expanded, so 5 rather than 6.

The student **explains** key methodological considerations: use of stratified random sampling at 2.5 m above Chart Datum, sample size justification via running mean, control of seasonal timing, micro-habitat, geology and tidal height, and explicit instrument precision.  Limitations such as variable sheltering and nutrition are also discussed.  These details go beyond merely stating or describing and show clear reasoning, though occasional gaps (e.g., no discussion of replication dates) prevent a perfect 6.

The apparatus list and **step-by-step procedure** (numbered 1â11) would allow another investigator to reproduce the study, including height determination with an optic level, random point generation, and calculation methods.  Minor ambiguities (software version, exact random number range) leave a small chance of confusion, so a solid 5 within the highest band is warranted.

annotations.comment: The research question is precisely stated and embedded within a clear ecological and geographical context, including specific exposure levels, organism, sampling period, and response variable, facilitating focused investigation.
Background description is comprehensive but could more directly link Ballantineâs scale values to quantitative wave energy metrics, strengthening the rationale for exposure differences.
The box-and-whisker interpretation effectively highlights IQR and spread differences, demonstrating clear and precise data processing communication.
The prediction is clear but would be strengthened by specifying an expected magnitude or range of ratio difference based on preliminary data or literature.
Independent and dependent variables are explicitly defined, including exposure levels and measurement precision, which enhances clarity and replicability.
Instrument uncertainty (Â±0.05 mm) is stated but not propagated into the width-to-height ratio or reflected in error bars; include uncertainty calculation in data processing.
Sampling rationale using stratified random sampling at 2.5 m above Chart Datum is well justified and reduces researcher bias by random organism selection.
Random point selection is described but lacks details on the random number generator parameters (seed, range); include this to ensure full reproducibility.
Controlled variables table comprehensively identifies key factors (time of year, microhabitat, geology, height) and describes how each is managed, demonstrating strong methodological consideration.
Uncontrolled variables are acknowledged, but consider quantifying microhabitat variability (e.g.	percent algal cover) and including it as a covariate in analysis to strengthen control discussion.
Apparatus list is detailed and precise, including instrument uncertainties, which supports replicability of the methodology.
Specify the make, model, and calibration procedure for the optic level to eliminate ambiguity and allow for exact replication.
Clarify how random points are distributed along the tape measure (e.g.	uniform distribution parameters) to strengthen the reproducibility of the random sampling step.
Map for Jetty Beach provides location context, but include the grid reference for Castle Beach as well to fully anchor both study sites.
Ethical considerations are addressed, but citing relevant invertebrate handling guidelines or permits would strengthen the ethical justification.
The risk assessment table systematically identifies hazards, scores, and control measures, demonstrating robust planning for field safety.
Initial qualitative observations are well integrated with quantitative findings, enhancing the narrative and supporting later analyses.
When referring to the image, explicitly cite âFigure 7â in the text to guide the reader directly to the corresponding qualitative illustration.
Raw data table is comprehensive but shows inconsistent comma/decimal notation; standardize to one style (e.g.	use decimal points) to improve precision and readability.
The narrative describes standard deviation error bars but does not incorporate instrument uncertainty; include propagated measurement error in graphical error bars.
Caption references âFigure 12â but the actual tally table content is missing; include the completed table data to support the box plot discussion.
Add a formal normality test (e.g.	ShapiroâWilk) before applying the t-test to justify the assumption of normal distribution.
The t-test summary table lists degrees of freedom as â18â instead of the correct â58,â which could mislead interpretation; revise the DF value to 58.
Interpretation of the test statistic (2.69 > 2.02) is articulated clearly and accurately connects to hypothesis rejection.
The conclusion correctly identifies a mean difference of 0.12 but mislabels it as âmmâ rather than a dimensionless ratio; revise unit notation.
Literature comparison to limpets is relevant but could be expanded with statistical findings or contrasting studies to deepen the scientific context.
The reflection on 95% certainty demonstrates thoughtful evaluation of statistical significance and supports the conclusionâs robustness.
Evaluation table lists specific methodological limitations with associated impacts and realistic improvements, demonstrating strong critical reflection.
Figure 16 caption appears without direct reference in the text; explicitly mention âFigure 16â in the narrative to enhance cross-referencing.
